  7→package kotlinx.datetime
  8→
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.InstantIso8601Serializer
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→import java.time.DateTimeException
 15→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
 16→import kotlin.time.*
 17→import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.nanoseconds
 18→import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.seconds
 19→import java.time.Instant as jtInstant
 20→import java.time.Clock as jtClock
 21→
 22→@Serializable(with = InstantIso8601Serializer::class)
 23→public actual class Instant internal constructor(internal val value: jtInstant) : Comparable<Instant> {
 24→
 25→    public actual val epochSeconds: Long
 26→        get() = value.epochSecond
 27→    public actual val nanosecondsOfSecond: Int
 28→        get() = value.nano
 29→
 30→    public actual fun toEpochMilliseconds(): Long = try {
 31→        value.toEpochMilli()
 32→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
 33→        if (value.isAfter(java.time.Instant.EPOCH)) Long.MAX_VALUE else Long.MIN_VALUE
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    public actual operator fun plus(duration: Duration): Instant = duration.toComponents { seconds, nanoseconds ->
 37→        try {
 38→            Instant(value.plusSeconds(seconds).plusNanos(nanoseconds.toLong()))
 39→        } catch (e: java.lang.Exception) {
 40→            if (e !is ArithmeticException && e !is DateTimeException) throw e
 41→            if (duration.isPositive()) MAX else MIN
 42→        }
 43→    }
 44→
 45→    public actual operator fun minus(duration: Duration): Instant = plus(-duration)
 46→
 47→    public actual operator fun minus(other: Instant): Duration =
 48→        (this.value.epochSecond - other.value.epochSecond).seconds + // won't overflow given the instant bounds
 49→            (this.value.nano - other.value.nano).nanoseconds
 50→
 51→    public actual override operator fun compareTo(other: Instant): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 52→
 53→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 54→            (this === other) || (other is Instant && this.value == other.value)
 55→
 56→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 57→
 58→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 59→
 60→    public actual companion object {
 61→        @Deprecated("Use Clock.System.now() instead", ReplaceWith("Clock.System.now()", "kotlinx.datetime.Clock"), level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
 62→        public actual fun now(): Instant =
 63→                Instant(jtClock.systemUTC().instant())
 64→
 65→        public actual fun fromEpochMilliseconds(epochMilliseconds: Long): Instant =
 66→                Instant(jtInstant.ofEpochMilli(epochMilliseconds))
 67→
 68→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>): Instant = try {
 69→            format.parse(input).toInstantUsingOffset()
 70→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 71→            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse an instant from '$input'", e)
 72→        }
 73→
 74→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 75→        public fun parse(isoString: String): Instant = parse(input = isoString)
 76→
 77→        public actual fun fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds: Long, nanosecondAdjustment: Long): Instant = try {
 78→            Instant(jtInstant.ofEpochSecond(epochSeconds, nanosecondAdjustment))
 79→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 80→            if (e !is ArithmeticException && e !is DateTimeException) throw e
 81→            if (epochSeconds > 0) MAX else MIN
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        public actual fun fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds: Long, nanosecondAdjustment: Int): Instant =
 85→            fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds, nanosecondAdjustment.toLong())
 86→
 87→        public actual val DISTANT_PAST: Instant = Instant(jtInstant.ofEpochSecond(DISTANT_PAST_SECONDS, 999_999_999))
 88→        public actual val DISTANT_FUTURE: Instant = Instant(jtInstant.ofEpochSecond(DISTANT_FUTURE_SECONDS, 0))
 89→
 90→        internal actual val MIN: Instant = Instant(jtInstant.MIN)
 91→        internal actual val MAX: Instant = Instant(jtInstant.MAX)
 92→    }
 93→}
 94→
 95→private fun Instant.atZone(zone: TimeZone): java.time.ZonedDateTime = try {
 96→    value.atZone(zone.zoneId)
 97→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
 98→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException(e)
 99→}

 6→package kotlinx.datetime

 8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.Instant as jtInstant
10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.Duration as jtDuration
11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.Clock as jtClock
12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.ChronoUnit as jtChronoUnit
13→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.ZonedDateTime as jtZonedDateTime
14→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
15→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
16→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.InstantIso8601Serializer
17→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
18→import kotlin.time.*
19→import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.nanoseconds
20→import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.seconds
21→
22→@Serializable(with = InstantIso8601Serializer::class)
23→public actual class Instant internal constructor(internal val value: jtInstant) : Comparable<Instant> {
24→
25→    public actual val epochSeconds: Long
26→        get() = value.epochSecond().toLong()
27→    public actual val nanosecondsOfSecond: Int
28→        get() = value.nano().toInt()
29→
30→    public actual fun toEpochMilliseconds(): Long =
31→            epochSeconds * MILLIS_PER_ONE + nanosecondsOfSecond / NANOS_PER_MILLI
32→
33→    public actual operator fun plus(duration: Duration): Instant = duration.toComponents { seconds, nanoseconds ->
34→        return try {
35→            Instant(plusFix(seconds.toDouble(), nanoseconds))
36→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
37→            if (!e.isJodaDateTimeException()) throw e
38→            if (duration.isPositive()) MAX else MIN
39→        }
40→    }
41→
42→    internal fun plusFix(seconds: Double, nanos: Int): jtInstant {
43→        val newSeconds = value.epochSecond() + seconds
44→        val newNanos = value.nano() + nanos
45→        return jsTry { jtInstant.ofEpochSecond(newSeconds, newNanos.toInt()) }
46→    }
47→
48→    public actual operator fun minus(duration: Duration): Instant = plus(-duration)
49→
50→    public actual operator fun minus(other: Instant): Duration {
51→        val diff = jtDuration.between(other.value, this.value)
52→        return diff.seconds().seconds + diff.nano().nanoseconds
53→    }
54→
55→    public actual override operator fun compareTo(other: Instant): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
56→
57→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
58→            (this === other) || (other is Instant && (this.value === other.value || this.value.equals(other.value)))
59→
60→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
61→
62→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
63→
64→    public actual companion object {
65→        @Deprecated("Use Clock.System.now() instead", ReplaceWith("Clock.System.now()", "kotlinx.datetime.Clock"), level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
66→        public actual fun now(): Instant =
67→                Instant(jtClock.systemUTC().instant())
68→
69→        public actual fun fromEpochMilliseconds(epochMilliseconds: Long): Instant = try {
70→            fromEpochSeconds(epochMilliseconds / MILLIS_PER_ONE, epochMilliseconds % MILLIS_PER_ONE * NANOS_PER_MILLI)
71→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
72→            if (!e.isJodaDateTimeException()) throw e
73→            if (epochMilliseconds > 0) MAX else MIN
74→        }
75→
76→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>): Instant = try {
77→            format.parse(input).toInstantUsingOffset()
78→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
79→            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse an instant from '$input'", e)
80→        }
81→
82→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
83→        public fun parse(isoString: String): Instant = parse(input = isoString)
84→
85→        public actual fun fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds: Long, nanosecondAdjustment: Long): Instant = try {
86→            /* Performing normalization here because otherwise this fails:
87→               assertEquals((Long.MAX_VALUE % 1_000_000_000).toInt(),
88→                            Instant.fromEpochSeconds(0, Long.MAX_VALUE).nanosecondsOfSecond) */
89→            val secs = safeAdd(epochSeconds, nanosecondAdjustment.floorDiv(NANOS_PER_ONE.toLong()))
90→            val nos = nanosecondAdjustment.mod(NANOS_PER_ONE.toLong()).toInt()
91→            Instant(jsTry { jtInstant.ofEpochSecond(secs.toDouble(), nos) })
92→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
93→            if (!e.isJodaDateTimeException() && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
94→            if (epochSeconds > 0) MAX else MIN
95→        }

  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.test
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 10→import kotlin.test.*
 11→
 12→class InstantParsingConsistencyTest {
 13→
 14→    @Test
 15→    fun testParseWithSecondsAndZOffset() {
 16→        // Seconds are required, should parse successfully
 17→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00Z")
 18→        assertEquals(2024, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).year)
 19→        assertEquals(3, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).monthNumber)
 20→        assertEquals(19, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).dayOfMonth)
 21→        assertEquals(17, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).hour)
 22→        assertEquals(50, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).minute)
 23→        assertEquals(0, instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC).second)
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    @Test
 27→    fun testParseWithoutSecondsShouldFail() {
 28→        // Seconds are required according to the spec, parsing should fail
 29→        assertFailsWith<DateTimeFormatException> {
 30→            Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50Z")
 31→        }
 32→    }
 33→
 34→    @Test
 35→    fun testParseWithSecondsAndNumericOffset() {
 36→        // Parsing with seconds and numeric offset should work
 37→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00+03:00")
 38→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
 39→        assertEquals(2024, utc.year)
 40→        assertEquals(3, utc.monthNumber)
 41→        assertEquals(19, utc.dayOfMonth)
 42→        assertEquals(14, utc.hour) // 17:50 +03:00 = 14:50 UTC
 43→        assertEquals(50, utc.minute)
 44→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    @Test
 48→    fun testParseWithSecondsAndOffsetWithoutMinutes() {
 49→        // Minutes of offset are optional, should parse successfully
 50→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00+03")
 51→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
 52→        assertEquals(2024, utc.year)
 53→        assertEquals(3, utc.monthNumber)
 54→        assertEquals(19, utc.dayOfMonth)
 55→        assertEquals(14, utc.hour) // 17:50 +03:00 = 14:50 UTC
 56→        assertEquals(50, utc.minute)
 57→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
 58→    }
 59→
 60→    @Test
 61→    fun testParseWithSecondsAndOffsetWithMinutes() {
 62→        // Offset with minutes should work
 63→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00+03:30")
 64→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
 65→        assertEquals(2024, utc.year)
 66→        assertEquals(3, utc.monthNumber)
 67→        assertEquals(19, utc.dayOfMonth)
 68→        assertEquals(14, utc.hour) // 17:50 +03:30 = 14:20 UTC
 69→        assertEquals(20, utc.minute)
 70→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    @Test
 74→    fun testParseWithFractionalSeconds() {
 75→        // Fractional seconds should work
 76→        val instant = Instant.parse("2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z")
 77→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
 78→        assertEquals(2020, utc.year)
 79→        assertEquals(8, utc.monthNumber)
 80→        assertEquals(30, utc.dayOfMonth)
 81→        assertEquals(18, utc.hour)
 82→        assertEquals(43, utc.minute)
 83→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
 84→        assertEquals(123456789, instant.nanosecondsOfSecond)
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    @Test
 88→    fun testParseWithNegativeOffset() {
 89→        // Negative offset should work
 90→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00-05:00")
 91→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
 92→        assertEquals(2024, utc.year)
 93→        assertEquals(3, utc.monthNumber)
 94→        assertEquals(19, utc.dayOfMonth)
 95→        assertEquals(22, utc.hour) // 17:50 -05:00 = 22:50 UTC
 96→        assertEquals(50, utc.minute)
 97→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    @Test
101→    fun testParseWithNegativeOffsetWithoutMinutes() {
102→        // Negative offset without minutes should work
103→        val instant = Instant.parse("2024-03-19T17:50:00-05")
104→        val utc = instant.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
105→        assertEquals(2024, utc.year)
106→        assertEquals(3, utc.monthNumber)
107→        assertEquals(19, utc.dayOfMonth)
108→        assertEquals(22, utc.hour) // 17:50 -05:00 = 22:50 UTC
109→        assertEquals(50, utc.minute)
110→        assertEquals(0, utc.second)
111→    }
112→}

 28→

1→/*

  5→package kotlinx.datetime.test
  6→
  7→import kotlinx.datetime.*
  8→import kotlinx.cinterop.*
  9→import platform.Foundation.*
 10→import kotlin.math.*
 11→import kotlin.random.*
 12→import kotlin.test.*
 13→
 14→class ConvertersTest {
 15→
 16→    private val dateFormatter = NSDateFormatter()
 17→    private val locale = NSLocale.localeWithLocaleIdentifier("en_US_POSIX")
 18→    private val gregorian = NSCalendar.calendarWithIdentifier(NSCalendarIdentifierGregorian)!!
 19→    private val utc = NSTimeZone.timeZoneForSecondsFromGMT(0)
 20→
 21→    init {
 22→        dateFormatter.locale = locale
 23→        dateFormatter.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZZZZZ"
 24→        dateFormatter.calendar = gregorian
 25→        dateFormatter.timeZone = utc
 26→    }
 27→
 28→    @Test
 29→    fun testToFromNSDate() {
 30→        // The first day on the Gregorian calendar. The day before it is 1582-10-04 in the Julian calendar.
 31→        val gregorianCalendarStart = Instant.parse("1582-10-15T00:00:00Z").toEpochMilliseconds()
 32→        val minBoundMillis = (NSDate.distantPast.timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000 + 0.5).toLong()
 33→        val maxBoundMillis = (NSDate.distantFuture.timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000 - 0.5).toLong()
 34→        repeat(STRESS_TEST_ITERATIONS) {
 35→            val millis = Random.nextLong(minBoundMillis, maxBoundMillis)
 36→            val instant = Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds(millis)
 37→            val date = instant.toNSDate()
 38→            // Darwin's date printer dynamically adjusts to switching between calendars, while our Instant does not.
 39→            if (millis >= gregorianCalendarStart) {
 40→                assertEquals(instant, Instant.parse(dateFormatter.stringFromDate(date)))
 41→            }
 42→            assertEquals(instant, date.toKotlinInstant())
 43→        }
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    @Test
 47→    fun availableZoneIdsToNSTimeZone() {
 48→        for (id in TimeZone.availableZoneIds) {
 49→            val normalizedId = (NSTimeZone.abbreviationDictionary[id] ?: id) as String
 50→            val timeZone = TimeZone.of(normalizedId)
 51→            if (timeZone is FixedOffsetTimeZone) {
 52→                continue
 53→            }
 54→            val nsTimeZone = try {
 55→                timeZone.toNSTimeZone()
 56→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 57→                assertEquals("America/Ciudad_Juarez", id)
 58→                continue
 59→            }
 60→            assertEquals(normalizedId, nsTimeZone.name)
 61→            assertEquals(timeZone, nsTimeZone.toKotlinTimeZone())
 62→        }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    // from threetenbp's tests for time zones
 66→    @Test
 67→    fun zoneOffsetToNSTimeZone() {
 68→        for (i in -18 * 60..18 * 60) {
 69→            val hours = i / 60
 70→            val minutes = i % 60
 71→            val str = (if (i < 0) "-" else "+") +
 72→                (abs(hours) + 100).toString().substring(1) + ":" +
 73→                (abs(minutes) + 100).toString().substring(1) + ":" +
 74→                "00"
 75→            val test = TimeZone.of(str) as FixedOffsetTimeZone
 76→            zoneOffsetCheck(test, hours, minutes)
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    @Test
 81→    fun localDateToNSDateComponentsTest() {
 82→        val date = LocalDate.parse("2019-02-04")
 83→        val components = date.toNSDateComponents()
 84→        components.timeZone = utc
 85→        val nsDate = gregorian.dateFromComponents(components)!!
 86→        val formatter = NSDateFormatter()
 87→        formatter.timeZone = utc
 88→        formatter.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd"
 89→        assertEquals("2019-02-04", formatter.stringFromDate(nsDate))
 90→    }

 92→    @Test
 93→    fun localDateTimeToNSDateComponentsTest() {
 94→        val str = "2019-02-04T23:59:30.543"
 95→        val dateTime = LocalDateTime.parse(str)
 96→        val components = dateTime.toNSDateComponents()
 97→        components.timeZone = utc
 98→        val nsDate = gregorian.dateFromComponents(components)!!
 99→        assertEquals(str + "Z", dateFormatter.stringFromDate(nsDate))
100→    }
101→
102→    @OptIn(kotlinx.cinterop.ExperimentalForeignApi::class)
103→    private fun zoneOffsetCheck(timeZone: FixedOffsetTimeZone, hours: Int, minutes: Int) {
104→        val nsTimeZone = timeZone.toNSTimeZone()
105→        val kotlinTimeZone = nsTimeZone.toKotlinTimeZone()
106→        assertEquals(hours * 3600 + minutes * 60, nsTimeZone.secondsFromGMT.convert())
107→        assertIs<FixedOffsetTimeZone>(kotlinTimeZone)
108→        assertEquals(timeZone.offset, kotlinTimeZone.offset)
109→    }
